title: Corporate Housing in Cleveland, Ohio: A Guide for Employers
metaTitle: Corporate Housing in Cleveland, Ohio: Employer Guide
metaDescription: Corporate housing Cleveland Ohio: a plain-English guide for employers relocating staff — what the term means locally and how our local rental team can help.
answerBox: Corporate housing usually means flexible, ready-to-move-in rentals for relocating or temporary staff. In Greater Cleveland, options range from furnished short-term suites to standard homes leased on regular terms. Rent Finder Cleveland is a local rental team, not a national relocation vendor. Tell us your headcount, timing, and target areas, and we'll say honestly what our current selection covers.

body: Corporate housing is a catch-all term for rentals set up to make an employee's move simpler than a standard 12-month lease. In practice it can mean a few different things: a fully furnished short-term suite billed to the company, an extended-stay unit near a job site, or an ordinary rental home leased on normal terms while a new hire settles in and decides where to live longer-term.

The right version depends on the assignment. A travel nurse on a 13-week contract needs something furnished and flexible. A household relocating permanently for a role usually just needs a real home to lease while they get their bearings. Knowing which situation you're solving for is the first step, because it changes who you should be talking to.

sections.h2: Corporate Housing Options in Greater Cleveland
body: Greater Cleveland has steady demand for temporary and relocation housing, driven by anchor employers like the Cleveland Clinic, University Hospitals, Progressive, Sherwin-Williams, and NASA Glenn, plus a large rotation of travel healthcare workers.

That demand is served by a mix of providers: national furnished-suite companies, extended-stay hotels, and — for anyone who needs a straightforward home rather than a hotel-style suite — the regular rental market across the East and Southeast side, several suburbs, and nearby Akron, Lorain, and Elyria.

Rents vary widely by location and bedroom count. HUD publishes Fair Market Rents for the Cleveland-Elyria metro each year, which is a useful reference point when you're budgeting a housing stipend or estimating what a two- or three-bedroom will run. Browse real examples under [houses for rent in Cleveland, OH](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/).

sections.h2: How Our Local Rental Team Can Help
body: [Rent Finder Cleveland](/cleveland-rentals/) is a local rental team. We help renters — including relocating employees — find, tour, and apply for homes we work with across Greater Cleveland. If your team needs standard rental homes on regular leases, that's squarely what we do, and we can move quickly because we already know which homes in our current selection are open.

For an employer, the practical value is speed and a single local contact. Instead of an employee cold-searching listing sites from another state, you send us the basics — how many people, when they need to be in, and the parts of the metro that fit their commute — and we tell you what's actually available now. Employees can [book a showing](/p/book-showing) directly when they're ready to look.

sections.h2: Being Honest About What We Are (and Aren't)
body: We want to be upfront so no one's time gets wasted. We are a local rental team — not a national relocation vendor, a corporate-suite operator, or a management company. We don't keep a furnished short-term inventory, we don't sell relocation packages, and we don't manage the homes we work with.

What we do have is a concentrated pool of roughly 90 rental homes across Cleveland's East and Southeast side, several suburbs, and Akron, Lorain, and Elyria — every one of which welcomes Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is kept ready for a HUD inspection. If a relocating employee happens to hold a voucher, that matters; see how it works on our [Section 8 housing in Cleveland](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) page.

One local note worth knowing: the city of Cleveland does not have a source-of-income ordinance, though a handful of inner-ring suburbs do — including Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale. We welcome vouchers everywhere we operate regardless, and we follow the Fair Housing Act in every interaction.

The fastest way to find out whether we're a fit is to send a short summary of the assignment. The more specific you are, the sooner we can tell you honestly what our current selection covers — and, just as importantly, where we can't help, so you can line up another provider without losing time.

items: Headcount and household sizes (how many homes, how many bedrooms each)
Move-in timing and how long each stay is expected to run
Target areas or must-be-near locations (a specific campus, downtown, a job site)
Whether furnished or unfurnished is required
A contact name and the best way to reach you

body: Reach our team at **(440) 444-4737** or **support@rentfindercleveland.com**, or start on the [corporate leasing page](/corporate-leasing/). Send the details above and we'll give you an honest read on what our current selection can cover for your incoming staff — with no cost or obligation to ask.

faq.q: Do you provide furnished corporate apartments?
faq.a: Not as a standing inventory. Our current selection is standard rental homes leased on regular terms. Tell us what you need and we'll say honestly whether we can help or point you elsewhere.
No. We're a local rental team that helps renters find, tour, and apply for homes we work with in Greater Cleveland. We don't sell relocation packages or manage properties.

faq.q: How many employees can you help house at once?
faq.a: It depends on what's open at the time. Send us your headcount and timing and we'll tell you how much of it our current selection can realistically cover.
Mainly Cleveland's East and Southeast side, several suburbs, and nearby Akron, Lorain, and Elyria. Share the campus or job site your team needs to be near and we'll match against what's available.

faq.q: Is there a cost to ask?
faq.a: No. There's no fee to tell us your team's needs and get an honest read on what we can cover.
